# Cryptographic Materials Generated by the OpenShift Installer

This document provides a comprehensive list of all cryptographic materials (certificates, keys, and CA bundles) generated by the OpenShift installer during cluster installation.

## Overview

The installer generates cryptographic materials in `pkg/asset/tls/` for securing cluster communications. All certificates use **RSA 2048-bit keys** and follow X.509 standards.

## Certificate Validity Periods

The installer uses three standard validity periods:

- **10 years** (`ValidityTenYears`): Long-lived CAs and certificates (Root CA, admin certs, kubelet bootstrap)
- **1 year** (`ValidityOneYear`): Control plane signers and client certificates
  - Reduced to **4 hours** when `ShortCertRotation` feature gate is enabled
- **1 day** (`ValidityOneDay`): API server serving certificates and short-lived signers
  - Reduced to **2 hours** when `ShortCertRotation` feature gate is enabled

## Certificate Authorities (CAs) and Signers

### 1. Root CA
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/root.go:19`
- **Common Name**: `root-ca`
- **Purpose**: Machine Config Server Root CA
- **Validity**: 10 years
- **Description**: Used to sign the MCS certificate. The private key is discarded after installation is complete.

### 2. Admin Kubeconfig Signer
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/adminkubeconfig.go:12`
- **Common Name**: `admin-kubeconfig-signer`
- **Validity**: 10 years
- **Purpose**: Signs admin kubeconfig client certificates

### 3. Aggregator Signer
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/aggregator.go:89`
- **Common Name**: `aggregator-signer`
- **Validity**: 1 day (2 hours with ShortCertRotation)
- **Purpose**: Signs aggregator client certificates for API server extension

### 4. Aggregator CA (Deprecated)
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/aggregator.go:14`
- **Common Name**: `aggregator`
- **Validity**: 1 day
- **Status**: Deprecated

### 5. Kube API Server to Kubelet Signer
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/apiserver.go:16`
- **Common Name**: `kube-apiserver-to-kubelet-signer`
- **Validity**: 1 year (4 hours with ShortCertRotation)
- **Purpose**: Signs certificates for kube-apiserver to kubelet communication

### 6. Kube API Server Localhost Signer
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/apiserver.go:113`
- **Common Name**: `kube-apiserver-localhost-signer`
- **Validity**: 10 years
- **Purpose**: Signs certificates for SNI localhost connections to kube-apiserver

### 7. Kube API Server Service Network Signer
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/apiserver.go:217`
- **Common Name**: `kube-apiserver-service-network-signer`
- **Validity**: 10 years
- **Purpose**: Signs certificates for SNI service network connections to kube-apiserver

### 8. Kube API Server Load Balancer Signer
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/apiserver.go:330`
- **Common Name**: `kube-apiserver-lb-signer`
- **Validity**: 10 years
- **Purpose**: Signs certificates for SNI load balancer connections to kube-apiserver

### 9. Kube Control Plane Signer
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/kubecontrolplane.go:13`
- **Common Name**: `kube-control-plane-signer`
- **Validity**: 1 year (4 hours with ShortCertRotation)
- **Purpose**: Signs control plane component client certificates

### 10. Kubelet CSR Signer
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/kubelet.go:13`
- **Common Name**: `kubelet-signer`
- **Validity**: 1 day (2 hours with ShortCertRotation)
- **Purpose**: Signs kubelet client certificates

### 11. Kubelet Bootstrap Cert Signer
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/kubelet.go:105`
- **Common Name**: `kubelet-bootstrap-kubeconfig-signer`
- **Validity**: 10 years
- **Purpose**: Signs kubelet bootstrap kubeconfig client certificates used to create CSRs for real certificates

## Server Certificates

### 12. Machine Config Server (MCS) Certificate
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/mcscertkey.go:19`
- **Common Name**: `system:machine-config-server`
- **Validity**: 10 years
- **Extended Key Usage**: Server Authentication
- **Subject Alternative Names**:
  - DNS names: Internal API address, API VIPs
  - IP addresses: API VIPs (platform-specific)
- **Purpose**: Secures the Machine Config Server endpoint

### 13. Kube API Server Localhost Server Certificate
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/apiserver.go:177`
- **Common Name**: `system:kube-apiserver`
- **Organization**: `kube-master`
- **Validity**: 1 day (2 hours with ShortCertRotation)
- **DNS Names**: `localhost`
- **IP Addresses**: `127.0.0.1`, `::1`
- **Purpose**: SNI localhost connections to kube-apiserver

### 14. Kube API Server Service Network Server Certificate
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/apiserver.go:281`
- **Common Name**: `system:kube-apiserver`
- **Organization**: `kube-master`
- **Validity**: 1 day (2 hours with ShortCertRotation)
- **DNS Names**:
  - `kubernetes`, `kubernetes.default`, `kubernetes.default.svc`, `kubernetes.default.svc.cluster.local`
  - `openshift`, `openshift.default`, `openshift.default.svc`, `openshift.default.svc.cluster.local`
- **IP Addresses**: First service network IP
- **Purpose**: SNI service network connections to kube-apiserver

### 15. Kube API Server External Load Balancer Server Certificate
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/apiserver.go:394`
- **Common Name**: `system:kube-apiserver`
- **Organization**: `kube-master`
- **Validity**: 1 day (2 hours with ShortCertRotation)
- **DNS Names**: External API address
- **Purpose**: SNI external load balancer connections to kube-apiserver

### 16. Kube API Server Internal Load Balancer Server Certificate
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/apiserver.go:433`
- **Common Name**: `system:kube-apiserver`
- **Organization**: `kube-master`
- **Validity**: 1 day (2 hours with ShortCertRotation)
- **DNS Names**: Internal API address
- **Purpose**: SNI internal load balancer connections to kube-apiserver

### 17. Ironic TLS Certificate
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/ironictls.go:17`
- **Common Name**: `ironic`
- **Validity**: 1 day (2 hours with ShortCertRotation)
- **Platform**: Bare Metal only
- **Purpose**: Secures virtual media TLS for Ironic in bare metal installations
- **DNS Names**: Internal API address
- **IP Addresses**: API VIPs, bootstrap provisioning IP

## Client Certificates

### 18. Admin Kubeconfig Client Certificate
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/adminkubeconfig.go:76`
- **Common Name**: `system:admin`
- **Organization**: `system:masters`
- **Validity**: 10 years
- **Extended Key Usage**: Server Authentication, Client Authentication
- **Purpose**: Admin kubeconfig authentication to kube-apiserver

### 19. Aggregator Client Certificate
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/aggregator.go:150`
- **Common Name**: `system:kube-apiserver-proxy`
- **Organization**: `kube-master`
- **Validity**: 1 day (2 hours with ShortCertRotation)
- **Purpose**: API server proxy for aggregation layer

### 20. API Server Proxy Certificate (Deprecated)
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/aggregator.go:51`
- **Common Name**: `system:kube-apiserver-proxy`
- **Organization**: `kube-master`
- **Validity**: 1 day
- **Status**: Deprecated

### 21. Kube API Server to Kubelet Client Certificate
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/apiserver.go:77`
- **Common Name**: `system:kube-apiserver`
- **Organization**: `kube-master`
- **Validity**: 1 year (4 hours with ShortCertRotation)
- **Purpose**: Kube-apiserver authentication to kubelet

### 22. Kube Controller Manager Client Certificate
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/kubecontrolplane.go:77`
- **Common Name**: `system:admin`
- **Organization**: `system:masters`
- **Validity**: 1 year (4 hours with ShortCertRotation)
- **Purpose**: Kube-controller-manager authentication to kube-apiserver

### 23. Kube Scheduler Client Certificate
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/kubecontrolplane.go:113`
- **Common Name**: `system:admin`
- **Organization**: `system:masters`
- **Validity**: 1 year (4 hours with ShortCertRotation)
- **Purpose**: Kube-scheduler authentication to kube-apiserver

### 24. Kubelet Client Certificate
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/kubelet.go:165`
- **Common Name**: `system:serviceaccount:openshift-machine-config-operator:node-bootstrapper`
- **Organization**: `system:serviceaccounts:openshift-machine-config-operator`, `system:serviceaccounts`
- **Validity**: 10 years
- **Purpose**: Kubelet client authentication to kube-apiserver (can be revoked by deleting the configmap containing its signer)

### 25. Journal Gateway Certificate
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/journalcertkey.go:13`
- **Common Name**: `journal-gatewayd`
- **Organization**: `OpenShift Bootstrap`
- **Validity**: 10 years
- **Extended Key Usage**: Server Authentication, Client Authentication
- **Purpose**: Authentication with journal-gatewayd on the bootstrap node

## CA Bundles

CA bundles combine multiple CA certificates into a single file for trust validation.

### 26. Admin Kubeconfig CA Bundle
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/adminkubeconfig.go:47`
- **Name**: `admin-kubeconfig-ca-bundle`
- **Contains**: AdminKubeConfigSignerCertKey

### 27. Aggregator CA Bundle
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/aggregator.go:121`
- **Name**: `aggregator-ca-bundle`
- **Contains**: AggregatorSignerCertKey

### 28. Kube API Server to Kubelet CA Bundle
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/apiserver.go:48`
- **Name**: `kube-apiserver-to-kubelet-ca-bundle`
- **Contains**: KubeAPIServerToKubeletSignerCertKey

### 29. Kube API Server Localhost CA Bundle
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/apiserver.go:148`
- **Name**: `kube-apiserver-localhost-ca-bundle`
- **Contains**: KubeAPIServerLocalhostSignerCertKey

### 30. Kube API Server Service Network CA Bundle
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/apiserver.go:252`
- **Name**: `kube-apiserver-service-network-ca-bundle`
- **Contains**: KubeAPIServerServiceNetworkSignerCertKey

### 31. Kube API Server LB CA Bundle
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/apiserver.go:365`
- **Name**: `kube-apiserver-lb-ca-bundle`
- **Contains**: KubeAPIServerLBSignerCertKey

### 32. Kube API Server Complete Server CA Bundle
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/apiserver.go:473`
- **Name**: `kube-apiserver-complete-server-ca-bundle`
- **Contains**: All CA certificates valid to confirm kube-apiserver identity
  - KubeAPIServerLocalhostCABundle
  - KubeAPIServerServiceNetworkCABundle
  - KubeAPIServerLBCABundle

### 33. Kube API Server Complete Client CA Bundle
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/apiserver.go:505`
- **Name**: `kube-apiserver-complete-client-ca-bundle`
- **Contains**: All CA certificates that kube-apiserver trusts for client authentication
  - AdminKubeConfigCABundle (admin.kubeconfig)
  - KubeletClientCABundle (signed kubelet certs)
  - KubeControlPlaneCABundle (controller-manager, scheduler)
  - KubeAPIServerToKubeletCABundle (kube-apiserver to kubelet)
  - KubeletBootstrapCABundle (used to create kubelet kubeconfig files for CSRs)

### 34. Kube Control Plane CA Bundle
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/kubecontrolplane.go:45`
- **Name**: `kube-control-plane-ca-bundle`
- **Contains**:
  - KubeControlPlaneSignerCertKey
  - KubeAPIServerLBSignerCertKey
  - KubeAPIServerLocalhostSignerCertKey
  - KubeAPIServerServiceNetworkSignerCertKey

### 35. Kubelet Client CA Bundle
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/kubelet.go:45`
- **Name**: `kubelet-client-ca-bundle`
- **Contains**: KubeletCSRSignerCertKey

### 36. Kubelet Serving CA Bundle
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/kubelet.go:75`
- **Name**: `kubelet-serving-ca-bundle`
- **Contains**: KubeletCSRSignerCertKey

### 37. Kubelet Bootstrap CA Bundle
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/kubelet.go:135`
- **Name**: `kubelet-bootstrap-kubeconfig-ca-bundle`
- **Contains**: KubeletBootstrapCertSigner

### 38. Cloud Provider CA Bundle
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/cloudprovidercabundle.go:16`
- **Name**: `cloud-ca-cert.pem`
- **Platform**: AWS secret regions only
- **Contains**: AdditionalTrustBundle from install config
- **Purpose**: Trusting communication with cloud provider (used by machine-config-operator on bootstrap node)
- **Condition**: Only generated for AWS secret regions when AdditionalTrustBundle is provided

### 39. BMC Verify CA Certificate
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/bmcverifyca.go:13`
- **Name**: `bmc-ca/verify_ca.crt`
- **Platform**: Bare Metal only
- **Type**: User-provided CA certificate
- **Purpose**: Verifies BMC (Baseboard Management Controller) TLS certificates for secure communication with bare metal hardware
- **Condition**: Only generated for bare metal platform when BMCVerifyCA is configured in install config
- **Note**: Does not generate new cryptographic material; loads user-provided CA certificate from install config

## Key Pairs (Non-Certificate)

### 40. Service Account Key Pair
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/serviceaccountkeypair.go:10`
- **Name**: `service-account.pub` / `service-account.key`
- **Type**: RSA public/private key pair
- **Purpose**: Service account token signing
- **Note**: Not written to disk during normal installation

### 41. Bootstrap SSH Key Pair
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/bootstrapsshkeypair.go:16`
- **Name**: `bootstrap-ssh.pub` / `bootstrap-ssh.key`
- **Type**: SSH RSA key pair
- **Purpose**: SSH access to bootstrap host
- **Format**: Private key (PEM), Public key (OpenSSH authorized_keys format)

### 42. Bound Service Account Signing Key
- **File**: `pkg/asset/tls/boundsasigningkey.go:17`
- **Name**: `bound-service-account-signing-key.key` / `bound-service-account-signing-key.pub`
- **Type**: User-provided RSA key pair (optional)
- **Purpose**: Service account signing key used by kube-apiserver
- **Note**: Only loaded when provided by user; does not generate new content

## Key Technical Details

### Key Generation
- All RSA keys use **2048-bit** key size (`keySize = 2048` in `pkg/asset/tls/tls.go:26`)
- Keys are generated using `crypto/rand.Reader` for cryptographic randomness

### Certificate Creation
- Self-signed certificates use `x509.CreateCertificate` with the certificate as both template and parent
- Signed certificates use `x509.CreateCertificate` with a CSR-based template and CA parent
- All certificates include `SubjectKeyId` generated from SHA-1 hash of the public key

### Storage Location
- Certificates and keys are stored in the `tls/` directory within installation assets
- Files use `.crt` extension for certificates and `.key` extension for private keys

### Post-Installation
- The Root CA private key is discarded after installation completes
- Many short-lived certificates are rotated by cluster operators after bootstrap

## References

- Machine Config Operator Certificates: https://github.com/openshift/api/tree/master/tls/docs/MachineConfig%20Operator%20Certificates
- Official Documentation: https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/latest/security/certificate_types_descriptions/machine-config-operator-certificates.html
